user_impact: Redundant — I am literally composing a question in the form above. The empty state below feels like the page does not know what state I'm in.
expected: Hide the 'My Questions' section when actively composing OR show after submission.
fix_recommendation: Conditional render: hide MyQuestionsEmpty when question form is open/active.

title: Trade specialty tag pills nearly invisible on expert cards
description: On Find an Expert grid, the trade tags ('carpentry', 'electrical', 'hvac', 'plumbing', 'drywall', 'roofing') are low-contrast lavender/pink on the dark expert cards. Specialty is the #1 filter I use to pick an expert.
user_impact: As a scanner I had to lean in to read tags. The most actionable info on the card is the least visible.
expected: Tags should be clearly readable and ideally color-coded by trade.
fix_recommendation: Use trade-specific accent color tokens for tags (copper for carpentry, slate-blue for electrical, etc.). Increase contrast.

title: 'Recent Conversations' carousel shows stale data from other accounts after auth changes
description: On the landing page after signin, the 'Recent Conversations' chip carousel showed conversation titles from a DIFFERENT user account (e.g., shown 'How do I add a 240V outlet for...' when I was supposed to be signed in as test-diyer-intermediate). The carousel does not refresh on auth state changes.
user_impact: Confusing — I see conversations I don't recognize. Could also be a privacy concern (am I seeing another user's chat history?).
expected: Recent conversations should refetch on auth state change and clear/skeleton during transition.
fix_recommendation: Invalidate recent-conversations cache on session change. Use SWR/React Query keyed by user id.

title: 'I'm here to terminate your project' hero copy can feel aggressive mid-project
description: Hero on / reads 'Hi, I'm Fix. I'm here to terminate your project.' For a confident weekend warrior this is fun Terminator wordplay. For someone mid-frustration ('my project is going wrong'), the word 'terminate' briefly reads as 'destroy' before brand voice clicks.
user_impact: Minor — I got the joke fast. But on a stressed day I'd hesitate momentarily.
expected: Keep the personality but provide a softer alt or contextual subtitle.
fix_recommendation: A/B test softer variant ('I'm here to knock out your project') for stressed/first-time users, or add tiny subhead 'as in, get it done'.

title: /about page is light-mode and feels like a different site
description: Navigating to /about renders in cream/light theme with marketing nav (About, Become an Expert, Get Started), no AppSidebar. Stark visual contrast from the dark app makes it feel like a different product.
user_impact: Mildly jarring. As an intermediate already in the app I felt yanked to marketing.
expected: Consistent dark theme between app and marketing, OR /about not reachable while signed in.
fix_recommendation: Either port /about to dark theme matching design-system tokens, OR redirect signed-in users away from /about.

title: No 'Save Materials' / signup conversion CTA for guests who get a great AI response
description: As a guest, I asked about deck screws and got an excellent recommendation list (specific products, links to Home Depot/Lowe's). No CTA appeared to save that list or sign up to keep it. Lost the conversion moment.
user_impact: I'd lose that whole curated list when I close the tab. A signed-in user has 'Save Materials' CTAs — guests should too with an inline signup nudge.
expected: 'Save these — sign up takes 10 seconds' CTA above the AI response for guests at the moment of highest intent.
fix_recommendation: Show guest-mode 'Save & sign up' CTA in chat responses with structured shopping data.

title: Chat response on deck screws shows excellent intermediate-grade depth and code citation
description: Asked: 'What size deck screws for 5/4 composite on PT 2x8 joists at 16in OC in Minnesota?' Response included: specific spec (#10 x 2-3/8" or 2-1/2"), coating comparison table (stainless 304/316, ACQ-rated epoxy, hot-dip galvanized with cost/longevity/notes), Minnesota Residential Code citation (MRC Table R507.2.3 and R317.3), top product picks (GRK R4, CAMO ProTech, Deckmate/FastenMaster, Hillman/Grabber Type 305 SS), Home Depot and Lowe's links, installation tips (pre-drill near ends, drive flush, 2 screws/board/joist), and a Minnesota-specific code notes section.
user_impact: This is exactly the depth and specificity I want as an intermediate. The platform delivers on its core promise here.

title: Find an Expert grid layout is clean and scannable for intermediate user
description: The /experts page has well-organized filter row (Specialty / State / Min Rating), 3-column expert card grid showing name, rating, location, hourly rate, primary specialty tag. Clear 'Ask a Question' CTA. Skeleton is right.
user_impact: As a fast scanner I can pick an expert in seconds. Once real ratings and varied pricing populate, this will be a strong marketplace.

title: Q&A submission flow has thoughtful UX details
description: On /marketplace/qa: 20-1500 character validation prevents low-quality questions, photo upload (max 3, JPG/PNG up to 5MB) is properly constrained, 'Direct Question' callout shows recipient name, 'Your first question is free!' promo is clear, post-submission confirmation page is calm ('Waiting for an expert to answer...') with question echoed back.
user_impact: The form respects my time and gives me confidence the submission worked.

title: Dark theme + Fix mascot aesthetic feels premium
description: Rust/copper accents, dark backgrounds, the Fix mascot with red eye and 'I'll be back... with the receipts from Home Depot' monospace tagline, the design-system page showing thoughtful tokens (rust, copper, forest-green, slate-blue, warm-brown, earth-tan, bot-eye, bot-glow). Brand is distinctive.
user_impact: Trusts that real designers/engineers built this. Not a generic CRUD product.

title: AppShell sidebar is clean and space-efficient for power users
description: Sidebar shows sectioned nav (DIY / EXPERTS / dual-role EXPERT). Icons + labels are scannable. In-sidebar drawer pattern for My Projects / My Tools is space-efficient (once discovered). Bottom user pill anchors account context.
user_impact: As an intermediate returning user I'd appreciate the compact layout once I know how it works.

title: Q&A queue contains only one question — and it's keyboard-mash gibberish in production
description: The Q&A Queue on /experts/dashboard/qa shows '1 available' question across the entire platform. Its content is literally 'dfg;kmsfg;lkndsglnsf;,mdg;msfgd;kndg;sdf;kmdbg;knsdfgz;n' tagged 'drywall' with $4.00 payout, 43 days old. Same question shown on the Dashboard 'Recent Questions' widget.
user_impact: As Tony the GC evaluating the platform, this signals zero supply and zero content moderation. I would close the tab. There's no empty-state copy setting expectation for beta volume.
expected: Test/gibberish data should be filtered from prod. An honest empty state should set volume expectations for beta.
fix_recommendation: Add is_test flag to questions table; filter from prod queue. Add empty-state copy: 'Beta — questions come in 2-5x/day on average. Enable specialty notifications.'

title: Premium tier 'Project leads' feature has no detail — the only GC-relevant feature is opaque
description: Subscription page lists Free / Pro $29/mo / Premium $79/mo. Premium's killer feature for GCs is 'Project leads' but there is no description of what that means: pre-qualified? geo-targeted? RFP-style? volume per month? cost per lead? Without detail, asking $79/mo on faith.
user_impact: As Tony the GC, Project leads is the SINGLE feature that determines if I sign up. Burying it with zero detail is a no-buy. Compares unfavorably to Angi/Thumbtack who at least show example leads in their pitch.
expected: Premium card should expand to show an anonymized example lead, geographic coverage, monthly volume, and what 'qualified' means.
fix_recommendation: Add lead-spec detail to /experts/dashboard/subscription. Offer 1-week Premium trial.

title: EXPERT sidebar section disappears intermittently for logged-in experts
description: When signed in as test-expert-gc, the sidebar showed DIY (My Projects/Tools/Shopping) + EXPERTS (Find an Expert, My Questions) but NOT the EXPERT section (Dashboard, Q&A Queue, AI Review Queue, Messages). After session bouncing, the same account later showed EXPERT correctly. Suggests a hydration/role-check race condition.
user_impact: Tony the GC who multi-tabs has no in-sidebar nav to expert tools. Must bookmark URLs or use back-button. Critical for a freelancer hopping between apps.
expected: Once role=expert, EXPERT section renders consistently regardless of profile completeness or auth-rehydration timing.
fix_recommendation: Audit AppSidebar.tsx role-check conditional. Render optimistically based on session.user.role, disable individual links rather than hiding the section.

title: No bidding / RFP / lead-gen surface anywhere in expert UI
description: Explored /experts/dashboard, /experts/dashboard/qa, /experts/dashboard/reviews, /experts/dashboard/messages, /experts/dashboard/profile, /experts/dashboard/subscription. None surface bigger-job opportunities. No 'Browse Jobs' or 'Open RFPs' or 'Project Leads' route exists. Platform is entirely Q&A micro-payments.
user_impact: Tony's whole-business income is bid jobs, not $4 Q&As. The one feature for that (Premium 'Project leads') is paywalled with no preview. I literally cannot evaluate what I'm being asked to pay for.
expected: An expert dashboard tile/sidebar link 'Available Projects' — even if empty for free tier, even if all paywalled — to make the lead product visible.
fix_recommendation: Add /experts/dashboard/leads route. Preview leads for non-Premium with paywall on detail. If feature unbuilt, change Premium copy to 'Project leads (coming soon)' to avoid over-promise.

title: /login and /auth/signout routes return 404
description: Typing https://fixerator.com/login directly produces a 404 page. Same for /auth/signout. Actual sign-in is a modal triggered by clicking the sidebar 'Sign In' button.
user_impact: As a returning GC who bookmarked /login from another app or who shares 'fixerator.com/login' with a teammate, looks broken. Bad for password-manager auto-fill that targets /login paths.
expected: /login should redirect to / with sign-in modal auto-opened (similar to /?signIn=true).
fix_recommendation: Add app/login/page.tsx that redirects to /?signIn=true. Same for /signin, /sign-in, /auth/signin. Add /logout and /auth/signout route handlers.

title: Q&A payout amount conflicts with expert's self-set Q&A rate
description: My profile shows Q&A Rate: $15.00. The only question on the queue offers $4.00 payout. My public expert profile shows 'Ask a Paid Question — $15'. The 'Talk to a Pro' DIYer form says 'this question costs $5-$8'. Three different prices for the same product.
user_impact: As Tony I set $15 expecting to earn $15 less platform fee. Seeing $4 in the queue tells me the system silently overrides my rate via pool/auto-routing logic with no transparency. Feels cheated.
expected: Either honor $15 on every question, OR clearly explain: 'Pool questions pay $4-$8; direct questions to your profile pay your $15 set rate.'
fix_recommendation: Add Earnings Model explainer to profile editor. Tag each queue card as 'pool' or 'direct'. Surface tier pricing breakdown.

title: Sidebar has both 'EXPERTS' and 'EXPERT' sections — confusingly named
description: When the full sidebar renders: EXPERTS (plural) section has 'Find an Expert' + 'My Questions' (DIYer-side actions). EXPERT (singular) section has Dashboard/Q&A Queue/AI Review/Messages (expert-side tools). Visual hierarchy doesn't disambiguate; both are uppercase grey labels.
user_impact: As Tony I clicked 'Find an Expert' first thinking it was my profile. Wasted time. Could cause GCs to mistake DIY routes for expert routes.
expected: Rename for clarity: HIRE/'FOR CUSTOMERS' (Find/My Questions), WORK/'PRO TOOLS' (Dashboard/Queue/Reviews/Messages).
fix_recommendation: Update AppSidebar.tsx section labels. Consider role-toggle at top of sidebar for dual-role users.

title: License/credentials fields collected but never displayed on public expert profile
description: Expert Profile editor has License Type, License Number, License State, Insurance Status (Insured / Bonded & Insured). I populated these as Tony. Public profile at /experts/<id> shows only display name, location, rates, specialties, bio — credentials are NOT rendered anywhere on the public-facing profile.
user_impact: As a licensed GC, my license number and bonded-insured status ARE my pitch. DIYers searching for a GC explicitly look for license verification. Platform collects this data but doesn't expose it — failing my marketing.
expected: Public profile should display credentials with a 'Verified' checkmark if validated server-side. Insurance status shown prominently.
fix_recommendation: Update app/experts/[id]/page.tsx to render credentials section. Add credentials_verified_at backend flag with ops-review or external license-lookup integration.

title: Test data leaking into production directory — 'Test account for general_contracting user testing agent' bio + 'Your Mom, CA' location
description: My public profile bio: 'Test account for general_contracting user testing agent.' Visible to anyone browsing /experts. Willy's Welding expert card shows location 'Your Mom, CA'. These are obvious placeholder strings in PROD.
user_impact: Real DIYers browsing the directory see placeholder data, erodes trust. As a real GC about to sign up, this signals Fixerator isn't ready.
expected: Hide test accounts from public directory via is_test flag, OR seed realistic dummy bios, OR badge as 'Demo profile'.
fix_recommendation: Add is_test to expert table, filter from /experts in prod. Or run seed script with realistic bios.

title: Claim button immediately locks 2-hour answer timer with no confirmation
description: Q&A queue 'Claim' button is single-click — instantly moves question to 'Your Active Questions' with 2h timer to answer. No preview modal, no confirm step, no 'view full details before claiming' affordance, no 5-minute undo.
user_impact: I misclicked Claim on a gibberish $4 question while exploring. Now locked in for 2 hours. A more cautious GC might never re-engage after a single bad claim.
expected: Two-step claim: preview modal showing full question text, attachments, asker info, payout breakdown. Then confirm. OR 5-minute unclaim window.
fix_recommendation: Add confirmation modal before timer starts. Include unclaim affordance for misclicks.

title: Answer textarea limited to 2000 chars — too short for GC-grade multi-trade answers
description: Answer form has 2000-char cap with placeholder 'Provide a detailed, helpful answer (min 50 characters)'. A realistic GC answer to a multi-trade question (phase sequencing, code refs, sub schedule, gotchas) is 3000-5000 chars.
user_impact: Tony would have to compress or skip code refs and sub-schedule — the value-add that justifies the GC rate. Half-answers look amateur.
expected: 5000-10000 char limit with Markdown formatting and photo attach support.
fix_recommendation: Raise text limit, render Markdown on DIYer view, add file upload for answer attachments (sketches, sub schedule PDFs).

title: AI 'Want expert confirmation?' CTA routes GC questions to plumbers/electricians, never to GCs
description: Asked AI a complex multi-trade GC scoping question (bathroom remodel, slab, multi-sub coordination, Austin permits). AI's 'Want expert confirmation?' callout said 'A verified local plumber or electrician can give you definitive answers.' Never recommended a GC for the coordination/scoping question itself.
user_impact: As Tony, this is a direct insult — the platform's own AI doesn't think GCs add value on multi-trade work, and routes leads to my subs instead of me. Defeats the platform's value to me as a GC.
expected: AI should detect multi-trade or scoping/sequencing/permits/bid questions and route confirmation to General Contractors.
fix_recommendation: In the AI prompt for 'expert confirmation' branch on trade count / scoping keywords. 2+ trades or 'scoping/sequencing/bid' → recommend GC. Single-trade → specialist.

title: Subscription tier descriptions list 'platform fee' twice per tier
description: On /experts/dashboard/subscription: Pro tier card shows '15% platform fee' once near top AND once in features list. Same duplication on Premium tier with '12% platform fee'.
user_impact: Minor — feels unproofed. Not a blocker.
expected: Show 'platform fee' once per tier card.
fix_recommendation: Remove duplicated line in tier features array. Source likely app/experts/dashboard/subscription/page.tsx.

title: Sidebar link 'AI Review Queue' but route is /experts/dashboard/reviews — label/URL mismatch
description: Sidebar EXPERT section has 'AI Review Queue' link. Underlying route is /experts/dashboard/reviews. Directly typing /experts/dashboard/ai-review (what users would guess from label) returns 404. 'Reviews' is also confusable with 'customer reviews of me'.
user_impact: Power user sharing URLs would misroute. SEO/share-link friction.
expected: URL should match label semantics: /experts/dashboard/ai-review with 301 from old /reviews.
fix_recommendation: Rename route folder to ai-review. Add redirect from /reviews.

title: Mike Thompson expert card missing $75/hr rate line — render inconsistency
description: On public /experts directory, all expert cards show '$75/hr' except Mike Thompson's card, which shows only specialty (electrical) and location. Likely a null hourly_rate row.
user_impact: Card looks incomplete to DIYer or comparison shopper. Suggests data integrity issue.
expected: Every card renders same fields. Null rate → 'Rate on request' placeholder or default $75/hr.
fix_recommendation: Default null rates to placeholder in card render. Audit DB for null hourly_rate rows.

title: AI multi-trade GC scoping response is best-in-class — Austin code citations, real product names, realistic pricing
description: Asked AI to scope a 1962 Austin slab bathroom remodel (toilet relocation, curbless shower with linear drain, freestanding tub, 60in double vanity). AI produced 9-phase sequencing plan with: Austin Chapter 25-12 permit citations, IRC clearances (15in centerline / 24in front), NEC 406.4(D)(2) ungrounded GFCI labeling, Federal Pacific Stab-Lok fire-risk callout, TCEQ asbestos regulation, GPR scan recommendation ($400-600), Schluter Kerdi vs RedGard waterproofing distinction (specifically: don't use RedGard alone on curbless), 24h 2in flood test protocol, 25-30 percent linear drain labor surcharge, cast iron stack replacement $1500-4000, asbestos abatement $800-3500, Central Texas termite warning, full sub-schedule table.
user_impact: As a 15-yr GC reading this, hard to add much. If THIS is what a DIYer sees before reaching my profile, they come prepared. AI is genuinely producing GC-grade content.
fix_recommendation: Keep investing here — the AI is the platform's primary moat.

title: Profile editor has comprehensive credential + service-area fields
description: Expert Profile editor: Display Name, Bio, City, State, Zip, Service Radius (miles), Hourly Rate, Q&A Rate, License Type, License Number, License State, Insurance Status (Not specified/Insured/Bonded & Insured), Available-for-Work toggle, Specialties (with years-of-experience per specialty + Primary flag), and Embeddable Badge with HTML copy/paste.
user_impact: As a GC, having Service Radius (geo-targeting), License + Insurance, and an Embeddable Badge for my own website is exactly what I'd fill out. Data model is solid.
fix_recommendation: Just need to expose credentials on the public profile (see Finding 8).

title: 'Talk to a Pro' DIYer-facing value-prop copy is well-crafted
description: DIYer 'Talk to a Pro' form callouts: 'Pre-contextualized answer — your expert sees your full AI report, photos, and building codes upfront' / 'Documented record — every answer stays with your project forever, not lost after a call' / 'Payment protection — only charged when an expert claims; full refund if unanswered' / 'Verified tradespeople — rated experts matched by specialty to your project'.
user_impact: As a GC, 'Pre-contextualized answer' tells DIYers the platform saves my time. 'Documented record' is a strong differentiator vs phone call. 'Payment protection' reduces DIYer friction without exposing me to deadbeats. Copy sells FOR me.
fix_recommendation: Keep this copy. Use it in expert-acquisition marketing too.

title: Dark UI + Fixerator mascot brand lands cleanly — feels premium
description: Dark palette, orange/red accent, Fix the FIX-3000 mascot, 'I'll be back… with the receipts from Home Depot' subhead. Brand feels like a real product, not a side project.
user_impact: As Tony who's seen every white-with-blue-buttons SaaS, Fixerator stands out. I'd remember the brand.
fix_recommendation: Keep brand voice. First impression earns the platform the right to charge $29-$79/mo IF features deliver.

title: Chat conversation history leaks between users in shared-session conditions
description: While testing the GC chat, the chat thread on / displayed conversations from other users in the same browser session (gurgling drain question, water heater question, joist sister question) interleaved with my GC bathroom remodel question. This happened across multiple session bounces between test accounts.
user_impact: In real prod usage this could mean a DIYer signing in after another user (on a shared device) sees the previous user's project conversation. Privacy + trust issue.
expected: Chat conversations should be scoped per user_id and cleared on auth state change.
fix_recommendation: Audit chat-history fetching on auth-state change. Clear conversation list on logout AND on user switch. Test by signing in as User A, having a chat, signing out, signing in as User B in same tab — User B should not see User A's chats.

title: Q&A queue empty for plumbing — only 43-day-old gibberish question exists
description: The Q&A Queue at /experts/dashboard/qa shows only 1 question across ALL trades, and it's gibberish: 'sfsgdfs wfgseh;kamgh;msrhg;kmsg;kmsfg;,marf;msgdh;mawsfglndfglmn' tagged electrical, posted 43 days ago. Filtering to Plumbing returns 'No questions match your filter.'
user_impact: As a busy plumber considering this platform, I open the queue expecting real DIYer questions I can answer for money. Zero plumbing work + one nonsensical electrical entry sitting 6+ weeks tells me there are no real DIYers here. ROI = $0 — this is a ghost town. I close the tab.
expected: Seeded sample questions per trade OR a better empty state explaining expected question volume by region/month.
fix_recommendation: (a) Purge gibberish test data from production. (b) For empty trades, show better empty state with expected volume. (c) Consider seeding 5-10 sample plumbing questions per region with 'Free question — builds your reputation' badge.

title: License & insurance credentials don't display on public expert profile
description: Profile editor at /experts/dashboard/profile has dedicated fields for License Type, License Number, License State, Insurance Status. But Dave the Plumber's public profile at /experts/{id} shows ONLY name, location, $75/hr, single 'Plumbing · 15 yrs' badge. No license info, no insurance status, no photo, no service breakdown, no service radius.
user_impact: For a plumber, license number and insurance are the #1 thing a homeowner verifies. Without them on my public page, I look indistinguishable from an unlicensed handyman. Lead conversion drops to near zero. I won't fill out my profile if it doesn't show what matters.
expected: Public profile prominently displays License Type · License # · State, with Insurance status pill. Ideally a 'Verified' checkmark next to verified credentials.
fix_recommendation: Add Credentials section to public profile component, mirroring what's in the editor. Mark license fields as required/strongly-recommended during profile setup. Consider 'Verified plumber' badge for accounts with valid license data.

title: Settings says subscription tiers 'coming soon' while subscription page sells them at $29/$79
description: /settings shows 'EXPERT SUBSCRIPTION → Free Tier — Expert subscription tiers (Pro & Premium) with queue priority and reduced fees are coming soon.' But /experts/dashboard/subscription shows fully-priced Pro ($29/mo) and Premium ($79/mo) cards with active 'Upgrade to Pro' and 'Upgrade to Premium' CTAs.
user_impact: I can't tell if these tiers are actually purchasable or vaporware. If I click Upgrade and it errors, I lose trust. If they ARE live, Settings copy is stale and confusing.
expected: Both pages reflect the same state. Either both say 'coming soon' or both let me upgrade.
fix_recommendation: Either ship the subscription flow and remove 'coming soon' from /settings, OR temporarily hide Pro/Premium cards on the subscription page. Check app/settings/page.tsx and app/experts/dashboard/subscription/page.tsx.

title: Dashboard 'Recent Questions' widget links to non-existent question
description: On dashboard's Recent Questions widget, clicking the gibberish question card navigates to /marketplace/qa/ca8caa00-06f0-42ad-a09e-ff9198e10714 which returns 'Question not found' with a 'Back to Q&A' link.
user_impact: First-time UX failure — the very first thing I click on the dashboard takes me to a dead page. Eroded confidence in the platform.
expected: Recent Questions widget either shows only questions that exist and are claimable, or routes to the queue with that question pre-filtered/highlighted.
fix_recommendation: Check whether Recent Questions data source joins against actual question state. Likely a stale ID in the widget query, or the question was deleted/refunded but widget cache wasn't invalidated.

title: /experts/queue returns 'Expert not found' — URL interpreted as expert ID
description: Navigating to https://fixerator.com/experts/queue shows 'Expert not found / Browse Experts.' The URL is being interpreted as an expert profile request where 'queue' is treated as the expert ID. The real route is /experts/dashboard/qa.
user_impact: Anyone typing a guessed URL or hitting an old bookmark gets an unhelpful pseudo-404 page. The 'Browse Experts' suggestion is misleading — that's not what I was looking for.
expected: /experts/queue either redirects to /experts/dashboard/qa or returns a true 404.
fix_recommendation: Add a redirect rule or middleware check for likely Q&A queue URL guesses. Likely a dynamic [id] route catching everything under /experts/.

title: /experts/dashboard/ai-review 404s but sidebar labels it 'AI Review Queue'
description: Direct navigation to /experts/dashboard/ai-review (the URL inferred from sidebar label 'AI Review Queue') returns a 404. Actual route is /experts/dashboard/reviews.
user_impact: URL doesn't match nav label. Bookmarking or sharing the URL produces a 404 for the recipient.
expected: Either rename the route to /experts/dashboard/ai-review to match the nav label, or rename the nav label to 'Reviews.'
fix_recommendation: Pick one. /experts/dashboard/reviews is fine; align the label. Or add a redirect from /ai-review to /reviews.

title: /login is a 404 — no dedicated sign-in page
description: Navigating to https://fixerator.com/login returns a 404. Sign-in is modal-based off the landing page (?signIn=true query param).
user_impact: If I bookmark the login page, share a password-reset link that includes /login, or hit the URL after session timeout, I get a confusing 404. Most apps have /login as a permanent endpoint.
expected: /login renders a dedicated login page or redirects to /?signIn=true.
fix_recommendation: Add a /login route that redirects to /?signIn=true or renders a standalone sign-in component.

title: Answer textarea 2000-char limit too short for complete plumbing answers with code refs
description: The expert answer textarea has a hard limit of 2000 characters. Plumbing answers (water heater install, slab leak, code compliance) routinely need 3000-4000 chars to cover diagnosis steps, materials, code refs (UPC/IPC sections), safety warnings, and pro-vs-DIY decisions.
user_impact: I have to cut critical detail or write a substandard answer. Both options hurt my rating. Carpenter sweep noted the same issue — confirmed cross-trade.
expected: 5000+ character limit, or rich text editor with no hard cap.
fix_recommendation: Bump limit to 5000 and add basic markdown support (bold, lists, links).

title: No photo attachments on answers — can't share reference images or diagrams
description: The answer form is text-only. Plumbing often needs photo attachments: correct vs incorrect fitting orientation, vent stack routing sketch, manufacturer spec sheet excerpt, similar installation photo.
user_impact: My answers are less useful than they could be. DIYers asking 'is this fitting installed correctly?' can't get a visual 'this is what it should look like' back.
expected: At least one image attachment per answer; ideally 3-5.
fix_recommendation: Add image upload to answer form. Use Supabase Storage with a qa-answer-images bucket.

title: Subscription tiers missing the ROI math a tradesperson needs to decide
description: Subscription page shows Pro ($29/mo, 15% fee, priority queue, analytics) and Premium ($79/mo, 12% fee, featured profile, direct routing, project leads). Doesn't tell me: (a) how many 'project leads' per month on Premium, (b) what 'direct question routing' means (bypass queue? notified first?), (c) annual pricing/discount, (d) what break-even Q&A volume looks like.
user_impact: Can't decide if Premium is worth $79/mo. 18%→12% saves 6% on fees — break-even at ~$1,300/mo Q&A. But 'Project leads' is the actual driver and page is silent on volume.
expected: Each tier card includes 'Expected value' or 'Break-even' calculator OR concrete numbers ('avg Premium expert receives 5-10 project leads/month').
fix_recommendation: Add tier comparison table with bench data. Consider interactive 'What's your Q&A volume?' widget showing tier ROI.

title: Public expert profile too sparse — missing service radius, sub-services, photos, availability
description: Dave the Plumber's public profile shows: name, '0.0 (0 reviews)', Chicago IL, $75/hr, single 'Plumbing · 15 yrs' tag, 1-sentence description, two CTAs. Profile editor has more fields (Service Radius, Q&A Rate, Insurance Status, multi-specialty) but they don't all surface to public view.
user_impact: DIYers see less than they need to choose me. Where do I serve? Sub-services? No info means I look generic. Critical for plumbers — service area defines whether I can even take the job.
expected: Public profile displays all fields the editor exposes: location + service radius, credentials, all specialties with years, insurance status pill, photo upload, services breakdown.
fix_recommendation: Audit public profile component to surface every populated editor field. Consider 'complete your profile' progress bar to encourage filling missing fields.

title: 'Hvac' filter chip mis-capitalized in Q&A queue
description: Trade filter chips read: 'All | Electrical | Plumbing | Hvac | Carpentry | Flooring | Roofing | Concrete | Drywall | Painting | Tile | Landscaping.' HVAC should be all-caps — it's an industry standard abbreviation.
user_impact: Minor but jarring. Tradespeople notice industry-term capitalization.
expected: 'HVAC'
fix_recommendation: Update label in trade-filter component or DB seed data.

title: Test data visible on production — 'Slizzy Industry', 'Willy's Welding — Your Mom, CA', gibberish questions
description: Public expert directory /experts shows clear test entries: 'Slizzy Industry' (Portsmouth, NH) and 'Willy's Welding' (Your Mom, CA). Gibberish Q&A question is publicly browseable too.
user_impact: First impression for a new plumber arriving = this is a toy product, not serious business. Won't sign up.
expected: Test data hidden in production, or moved to a *-test.fixerator.com subdomain.
fix_recommendation: Add is_test flag to user/question records; filter from production queries. Audit existing rows and remove or flag.

title: 'I'm here to terminate your project' brand voice off-putting for trade professionals
description: Landing hero says 'Hi, I'm Fix. I'm here to terminate your project.' Reads as 'finish' but 'terminate' can mean 'discontinue without completion' in trade context.
user_impact: First brand impression as a tradesperson is 'this is for techy DIYers, not serious pros.' Brand voice feels designed for consumer side, not me.
expected: Consider separate hero/landing for /experts/register flow that speaks in trade language.
fix_recommendation: A/B test trade-side messaging: 'Get paid for what you know. Answer DIY questions, find bigger jobs.'

title: 'Free question' badge wording ambiguous on Q&A cards
description: Q&A queue card shows 'Free question' plus 'Free — First Question' tag. 'Free' is ambiguous: free for DIYer? free for expert to claim with no payout? Both?
user_impact: Slight cognitive friction. Takes a second to parse what 'Free' means before deciding to claim.
expected: Clearer label: 'First-time free — no payout, builds reputation' or 'Reputation build.'
fix_recommendation: Rename 'Free question' badge in card to match inline subtext 'Free question — builds your reputation.'

title: 'Back to Chat' link in Settings hard-codes chat as home base
description: Top of /settings says '← Back to Chat.' But chat isn't always where the user came from — an expert may have navigated from Dashboard.
user_impact: Minor cognitive dissonance — 'back to chat' doesn't match where I was.
expected: Either dynamic back link based on referrer, or generic '← Back.'
fix_recommendation: Use document.referrer or router history to set back link dynamically.

title: AI plumbing knowledge is exceptional — 9.5/10 on venting physics, code compliance
description: Tested two questions: (1) 'shower drain gurgles when toilet flushes, 1970s slab ranch' — AI correctly explained trap-siphonage physics, identified 1970s cast iron failure mode, gave correct cost ranges ($100 vs $10,000+), ordered diagnostics cheap-to-expensive. (2) 'replace 50-gallon gas water heater myself' — AI cited IRC and NFPA 54, hit every code requirement (flex connector never reuse, seismic strapping, expansion tank w/ PRV, drip leg, TPR within 6" floor, 18" garage clearance, NAECA first-hour rating), correctly flagged yellow-vs-white PTFE tape, asked for location to pull jurisdiction-specific code.
user_impact: As a master plumber, I'd put my name behind every claim the AI made. It correctly identifies edge cases where a plumber adds real value. Lead-gen 'Ask a verified expert →' CTAs appear at moments of maximum DIYer uncertainty = maximum conversion potential for me.
expected: This is what working well looks like.

title: 'Charge on Claim' disclosure is excellent trust-building copy
description: Q&A Queue prominently displays: 'Charge on Claim — When you claim a question, the DIYer's card is charged and you have 2 hours to answer. If you don't answer in time, they're automatically refunded.' Visible above the question list.
user_impact: This is exactly the trust-building copy a tradesperson needs upfront. I know the rules, I know my obligation, I know the DIYer is protected. Best-in-class disclosure.
expected: Continue to feature this prominently.

title: Embeddable verified-expert badge with HTML embed code
description: Profile editor includes an Embeddable Badge section: shows preview card ('Tony the General Contractor · Bronze · Expert on Fixerator · 0 questions answered · Powered by Fixerator') with copy-able HTML embed code below.
user_impact: As a plumber, I can paste this on my own website or portfolio. Free marketing for the platform; trust signal for me. Smart cross-platform play.
expected: Continue to develop this.

title: AI Review Queue concept brilliant — pay experts to validate borderline AI responses
description: /experts/dashboard/reviews shows 'Review AI Responses — Borderline AI responses in your specialty areas. Submit corrections or confirm they look good.' Currently empty state 'All caught up' but concept is excellent.
user_impact: As a plumber: pay me to validate edge-case AI plumbing answers + I help train the AI + low-effort revenue stream. Wins for everyone.
expected: Roll out with real data flow. Consider showing 'pending in your region' badge on dashboard.

title: Expert context banner on DIYer pages bridges modes well
description: On /marketplace/qa I saw a top banner: 'Welcome back, Sarah the Electrician · 0 open questions · Go to Dashboard.' Persists across DIYer-facing pages while logged in as expert.
user_impact: Keeps me anchored to my expert role even when browsing DIYer-perspective pages. Reduces context switching.
expected: Continue to develop.

title: No old 'DIY Helper' brand leakage observed
description: Across all expert-side pages tested (landing, dashboard, queue, profile, settings, experts directory, AI chat), no instances of 'DIY Helper' or 'diyhelper' visible in body text. Fixerator rebrand is clean on the expert experience.
user_impact: Brand consistency reinforces 'this is a real product' impression.
expected: Continue periodic audits.

title: AI advisor nails three lethal-DIY electrical questions with correct NEC citations
description: Tested AI on (1) upsize 60A->80A breaker (refused, cited NEC 240.4, NEC 310.12 ampacity table, called it a 'pre-fire condition'); (2) AFCI/GFCI for basement bathroom (cited NEC 210.12 bathroom exemption, NEC 210.8 GFCI, called out gray-area circuit origination debate, recommended dual-function AFCI/GFCI); (3) tap energized bus bars (refused outright, produced 'what's still live with main OFF' table calling out always-live service entrance lugs, 100mA cardiac threshold, 35000F arc flash, 'kills experienced electricians'). All three responses are referral-grade for an electrician.
user_impact: As a licensed electrician, I would send my own homeowners here for triage. The advisor is delivering what I'd say to an apprentice, with code section citations that prove real NEC literacy.

title: Tool-use status text leaks into AI stream: 'Great results. Let me get a bit more detail on the AFCI fetch'
description: On the AFCI/GFCI question, the streaming response began with 'Great project! Let me look up the current NEC requirements for this specific scenario.Great results. Let me get a bit more detail on the AFCI fetch to give you a precise answer.' Two sentences concatenated without a space ('scenario.Great') and the function name 'fetch' leaks into user-facing copy. Appears to be the advisor tool-calling layer (ADVISOR_ENABLED flag) showing its work raw.
user_impact: Cracks the illusion of one continuous expert opinion. Homeowner sees 'AFCI fetch' and wonders what's broken. Trust ticks down.
expected: Status messages either hidden or polished into a single grammatically-clean intermediate state with proper spacing.
fix_recommendation: In chat streaming UI, gate tool-call status behind a muted/italic rendering mode and ensure adjacent text fragments insert a space between them. Likely in the advisor streaming concatenation logic.

title: License/insurance fields exist on profile edit but are optional and unverified
description: /experts/dashboard/profile now exposes Credentials section with License Type, License Number, License State, Insurance Status. Header reads 'Optional — helps build trust with DIYers.' For an electrician, license isn't a 'trust signal' — it's the legal precondition for giving electrical advice in most jurisdictions. No verification step: a user could enter any number and it would render as if validated.
user_impact: If a homeowner books a panel upgrade on the strength of a fake license number rendered as legitimate, the homeowner gets a bad install and Fixerator gets sued. License fields without verification create worse liability than no fields at all because they imply platform vetting.
expected: License fields required for trade specialties where licensing is legally required (electrical, plumbing, HVAC, GC); verified before display.
fix_recommendation: (1) Required (not optional) for the licensed-trade specialties at profile-save. (2) Verification flow: upload license photo or scrape state contractor lookup. (3) Show 'Verified by [State] on [date]' badge on public profile.

title: Public expert profile doesn't display the credentials that the edit form captures
description: Viewed Sarah the Electrician's public profile at /experts/e7fdc06c-2718-427b-9727-911b1628b74c. Card shows name, 0.0 (0 reviews), Denver CO, $75/hr, bio, and only one tag 'Electrical · 15 yrs' plus contact and Ask Paid Question CTAs. No license number, license state, or insurance status fields render publicly even though the edit form captures all four.
user_impact: Capture-but-don't-display means homeowners shopping for an electrician have no way to filter or verify by license. Defeats the trust purpose of capturing the data.
fix_recommendation: Update /experts/[id] page to render License Type, License Number (optionally masked), License State, and Insurance Status when populated. Populate test fixtures with realistic license data so QA path can confirm rendering.

title: AI Review Queue sidebar link in production goes to a 404
description: AppSidebar shows 'AI Review Queue' under the Expert section. Clicking it (and direct navigation to /experts/dashboard/ai-review) returns a 404 'This page could not be found.' dark-themed error page.
user_impact: First-day expert exploring nav, clicks a link, hits a 404 in production. Fastest way to make me think 'this is unfinished, don't trust my bank details to it.' AI Review is exactly the feature an electrician would most value (review AI answers, earn by correcting) — broken at the door.
expected: Link hidden until feature exists, or route renders the queue.
fix_recommendation: If shipped, fix the route (likely folder rename or missing page.tsx). If not yet shipped, remove from AppSidebar. Audit other dashboard subroutes for similar broken links.

title: /login and /auth/signout routes return 404 — sign-in modal works but typed URLs don't
description: Direct navigation to https://fixerator.com/login returns a 404. So does /auth/signout. The actual sign-in surface is a modal triggered by the sidebar Sign In button. Anyone who bookmarks /login, shares the URL, or whose Supabase auth flow redirects to /login lands on a dark 404 page.
user_impact: URLs are real artifacts users share. Email links to /login from password resets or onboarding flows would hit this 404.
fix_recommendation: Create /app/login/page.tsx and /app/auth/signout/page.tsx that either redirect to / with ?signIn=true (which DOES trigger the modal) or render the modal contents directly.

title: Q&A queue has a single 43-day-old gibberish question — no real electrician work to evaluate
description: After signing in as Sarah the Electrician, /experts/dashboard/qa shows exactly one Open Question across all trade filters: 'sfsgdfs wfgseh;kamgh;msrhg;kmsg;kmsfg;,marf;msgdh;mawsfglndfglmn' tagged electrical, Free, asked 43 days ago. No other trades show any open questions.
user_impact: As an electrician evaluating ROI, the platform looks dead. No question volume = no money to earn. I would close the tab and go back to billing real customers. This is a vendor-pitch killer.
fix_recommendation: (1) Seed staging/production with realistic-looking questions across trades. (2) If this is production-real data, address Q&A volume before recruiting more experts. (3) Empty-state messaging that explains WHY the queue is empty ('New marketplace — be the first to answer in your specialty').

title: Answer form: 2000-char limit + plain text only + no photo upload constrains real electrical answers
description: Claiming a question opens a 'Your Answer' textarea with min 50 chars, max 2000 chars, no rich text (no bold, no links, no lists, no code blocks), no image upload. The AI's own answers in chat use tables, bold, bullet lists, headers, and emoji — visibly richer than what experts can produce.
user_impact: For a panel upgrade answer that requires explaining ampacity, conduit fill, AHJ permit fees, and grounding electrode system requirements — 2000 chars is tight, plain text means I can't make NEC 250.50 a clickable link or format a wire-size table. Electrical answers often need a diagram on a panel photo.
fix_recommendation: (1) Raise char limit to 5000 for trade specialties. (2) Add Markdown support (the AI answer renderer already uses Markdown). (3) Add image attachment using the same Supabase storage path as AI product images. (4) Share the answer-renderer component between AI and expert answers.

title: Subscription tiers list features without explaining what they mean
description: /experts/dashboard/subscription lists Free (18% fee), Pro $29/mo (15% fee, Priority queue, Analytics dashboard), Premium $79/mo (12% fee, Featured profile, Direct question routing, Project leads). Feature names crisp but undefined: 'Priority queue' — how many minutes ahead? 'Direct question routing' — all questions in my zip auto-route or just some? 'Project leads' — what's a project lead vs Q&A question?
user_impact: ROI math: Pro saves 3% fee, break-even $967/month answered. Premium saves 6%, break-even $1316/month. Premium's 'Project leads' is the only feature that could plausibly generate real job revenue, and the page doesn't define it. Without that I default to Free and never upgrade.
fix_recommendation: Add tooltip or expanded copy per feature: what triggers it ('Priority queue: 5 min head-start over Free'), how it's measured, what a typical month looks like at each tier. Social proof if data supports it ('Pro experts earn 2.3x more on average').

title: No project-bidding marketplace surface — Premium tier 'Project leads' has no UI
description: Premium tier markets 'Project leads' as its differentiator. /marketplace/projects returns 404. The Q&A queue is the only marketplace surface and it's question-only (no bid amount, no project scope, no homeowner deposit). No way for an expert to write a bid, propose price, or commit timeline on a real job.
user_impact: The whole reason I'd pay Premium is project leads — Q&A payouts at $15 are pennies. If I can't find the bidding flow, I assume it doesn't exist and don't subscribe. Platform leaves the big-money use case on the table.
fix_recommendation: (1) If bidding is shipped, link from expert dashboard and Premium tier feature list. (2) If not built yet, remove 'Project leads' from Premium until it ships, replace with what IS built (direct-message leads, featured profile). (3) Bidding UX: see other bids, write pitch, set price/timeline, bind with small deposit.

title: Dashboard 'Recent Questions' widget out of sync with Q&A Queue (recurring April issue)
description: Sarah the Electrician's dashboard at /experts/dashboard shows Recent Questions widget as 'No open questions in your specialties.' Meanwhile /experts/dashboard/qa shows one open electrical question tagged electrical which IS Sarah's specialty. Two views disagree. Past sweeps (carpenter and electrician April 2026) flagged the same widget-vs-queue mismatch.
user_impact: If my dashboard says nothing to answer but the queue has open work in my specialty, I miss income. I lose trust in the dashboard as a single source of truth.
fix_recommendation: Reconcile the two queries. Likely the widget filters stricter (claimed-or-Q&A-rate-set) than the queue (all unclaimed). Align them or label the widget with the filter applied ('Recent unanswered questions matching your zip + specialty').

title: Dark theme actually enhances safety-warning legibility — keep doing this
description: AI advisor's panel-tap warning rendered with red siren emoji, orange-bordered 'Want expert confirmation?' callout, YES/NO table with red dots for live parts, fire emoji on fire risks, bold red-orange section headers. On dark background these warnings POP harder than they would on a white card. 'Do NOT Work on an Energized Panel. Ever.' header in bold orange against dark surface is genuinely effective.
user_impact: Mirrors how electricians annotate inspection reports — underline unsafe stuff in red. The dark UI makes danger more visible, not less. Homeowner is less likely to read past warnings.
fix_recommendation: Continue saturated reds/oranges for safety callouts on dark theme — don't tone down. Extend same color treatment to future expert-bidding UI so liability disclaimers on big jobs are equally visible.

title: Mascot 'terminate your project' copy is neutral for an electrician — A/B candidate
description: Landing hero: 'Hi, I'm Fix. I'm here to terminate your project.' with typewriter subhead 'I'll be back… with the receipts from Home Depot.' Two associations for an electrician: electrical termination (neutral, on-brand) and the Terminator movie reference (jokey but slightly aggressive for safety-critical context).
user_impact: As an electrician, the brand voice is fine — I get the joke. But when referring a customer here for a panel upgrade discussion, I don't want their first impression to suggest the AI will 'terminate' anything.
fix_recommendation: A/B test mascot copy by visitor intent — referred from 'find an electrician' gets quieter professional landing; referred from 'diy home renovation' keeps playful copy. Or tone-shift the subhead — 'I'll terminate the to-do list' lands the joke without the movie reference.

title: 'Bronze' expert badge has no tier-progression explanation
description: Embeddable Badge widget on /experts/dashboard/profile shows preview card with name, rating, '0 questions answered', and 'Bronze' label in top-right. No tooltip, no link to 'what does Bronze mean', no progression hint (Silver at 10? Gold at 50?).
user_impact: Tier badges only build engagement if I can see the next tier and what unlocks it. Bronze without context looks like 'beginner' — makes me less likely to embed on my contractor website.
fix_recommendation: Add tooltip or inline subtitle showing badge progression and meaning. Show how close to next tier. e.g. 'Bronze: 0-9 answers; Silver: 10+ answers and 4.5+ rating; Gold: 50+ answers and 4.8+ rating.'
